text: When you write code, you will often need to fix mistakes. Identifying bugs is the first step. You can look for errors by reading your code carefully or by testing small parts. A systematic approach is much more effective than guessing. One good method is to check one section of code at a time. You can use debugging tools like breakpoints, which pause the program so you can inspect values. Another helpful technique is logging, where you print messages to see what your program is doing. These strategies make finding and fixing problems faster and less frustrating.
